candidates.title: Unified Dynamic Weight Decay: A Proximal-Theoretic Framework with Standardized Evaluation and Comprehensive Benchmarking
candidates.summary: Unifies all four WD sub-approaches (scheduling, alignment-aware, decoupled, norm-matched) as instances of a time-varying proximal operator with per-parameter modulation function Phi. Provides proximal-theoretic taxonomy, WD Stability Condition, three standardized evaluation metrics (BEM/CSI/AIS), and the first compute-controlled head-to-head benchmark of all major WD variants. CWD falsification battery tests alignment-awareness vs. reduced effective WD. Integrates three iterations of empirical evidence into a coherent theoretical narrative. Predicts spatial modulation is the load-bearing axis while temporal scheduling is a red herring.
hypotheses: H1: All four WD sub-approaches expressible as time-varying proximal operators
H2: WD Stability Condition predicts warmup necessity and schedule instability
H3: Budget equivalence holds at scale (CIFAR + ImageNet)
H4: CWD's improvement >50% attributable to reduced effective WD strength
H5: CSI and AIS have predictive power (Spearman rho > 0.5) for method rankings
H6: Spatial modulation > temporal scheduling under budget equivalence
H7: Temporal + directional compose beneficially; directional + norm-matched are redundant
candidates.pilot_focus: CIFAR-10/ResNet-20: validate CSI/AIS differentiation, test CWD effective-lambda hypothesis, verify budget equivalence for cosine/SWD schedules
key_risks: Theory perceived as 'just notation' without predictive power
CWD falsification ambiguous at CIFAR scale
Metrics uninformative after controlling for WD strength
Soft CWD proximal approximation too crude
differentiators: vs. D'Angelo et al. NeurIPS 2024: We compare dynamic WD methods and propose metrics; they study WD mechanisms
vs. CWD ICLR 2026: We provide the first falsification battery and unified context
vs. Wen et al. 2025: We focus specifically on WD variants, not entire optimizers
vs. our iterations 0-2: Elevates from negative-result AADWD study to unified framework contribution

candidates.title: Rethinking Dynamic Weight Decay: Mechanism Decomposition Reveals When and Why Temporal Scheduling Fails While Spatial Modulation Succeeds
candidates.summary: Purely contrarian-empirical paper. Combines budget equivalence verification at scale with CWD mechanism decomposition and effective-LR equivalence testing. Minimal theory, maximal experimental rigor. Clean message: stop scheduling WD temporally; instead focus on per-layer/per-parameter modulation and understand you're adjusting effective LR.
hypotheses: Budget equivalence holds for all temporal schedules under mean-matching
CWD works by reducing effective WD, not through alignment-awareness
Effective-LR-matched constant WD reproduces CWD's performance
Critical period CWD captures most of full CWD's benefit
candidates.pilot_focus: Budget equivalence + CWD vs matched-lambda on CIFAR-10
key_risks: Pure negative result may face publication resistance
CWD's ablation already showed random mask underperforms (partial contradiction)
Effective-LR equivalence may not hold for parameters without normalization
differentiators: First head-to-head benchmark with null hypothesis testing
CWD falsification with matched-lambda (not done in original paper)
Critical period interaction analysis connecting Golatkar et al. 2019

candidates.title: Allostatic Weight Decay: A Multi-Timescale Regulation Framework Inspired by Synaptic Homeostasis and Thermodynamic Process Optimization
candidates.summary: High-novelty interdisciplinary proposal. Maps biological synaptic regulation hierarchy to a three-level adaptive WD controller: fast reactive (per-parameter alignment), medium adaptive (norm tracking), slow predictive (anticipatory scheduling). Adds periodic 'sleep phase' WD boost derived from neuroscience. Thermodynamic process optimization provides the mathematical language. Highest novelty but highest risk.
hypotheses: Multi-timescale allostatic WD outperforms all single-mechanism approaches
Periodic WD boost (10-20% duty cycle) improves weight norm stability >20%
Anticipatory component shows most benefit during LR transitions
Three-level hierarchy shows emergent benefit beyond sum of individual levels
key_risks: Many hyperparameters (3 levels x 2-3 params each)
Biological analogy may be seen as superficial by ML reviewers
Small improvement margins despite framework complexity
Thermodynamic ideal gas analogy may break for anisotropic dynamics
differentiators: 0 arXiv results for 'allostatic weight decay' -- genuinely novel framing
Periodic WD boost has no precedent in deep learning
Subsumes thermodynamic framework as special case of Level 3

decision_rationale: The front-runner (Unified Framework) is selected because it occupies the most defensible position: it combines theoretical novelty (proximal-operator unification, WD Stability Condition), empirical rigor (compute-controlled benchmark, CWD falsification), and practical tooling (standardized metrics, visualization). It incorporates the strongest elements from all six perspectives: the innovator's Phi-function unification, the pragmatist's diagnostic toolkit and feasible experiment plan, the theoretical's proximal characterization and stability analysis, the contrarian's budget equivalence emphasis and CWD skepticism, the interdisciplinary's thermodynamic vocabulary (as supplementary context), and the empiricist's rigorous statistical protocol. The contrarian mechanism paper (backup 1) is retained as a fallback if theory proves difficult; the allostatic WD (backup 2) is retained as a high-risk pivot if the front-runner is scooped.

perspectives_weighted.innovator: HIGH. The Phi-function unification, spectral alignment, and warm-start composition predictions are adopted as the framework's mathematical backbone. The Fisher-Rao extension is deferred to future work.
perspectives_weighted.pragmatist: HIGHEST. The entire experimental infrastructure (single codebase, phased benchmark, visualization toolkit), the six-panel diagnostic design, the implementation details (CWD one-liner, SWD adapter), and the risk-mitigation schedule are adopted wholesale. This perspective provides the paper's practical value.
perspectives_weighted.theoretical: HIGH. The proximal-operator characterization, WD Stability Condition, optimal quadratic schedule, and Bregman divergence bounds form the theoretical skeleton. The spectral alignment metric is simplified to AIS. The composition theorem (orthogonal vs redundant modulation) is a key prediction.
perspectives_weighted.contrarian: CRITICAL. The contrarian's budget equivalence emphasis, CWD skepticism, and 'spatial > temporal' thesis shape the paper's central narrative. The CWD falsification battery is the single highest-impact experiment. The warning that the unified framework may be solving a non-problem on the temporal axis is taken seriously and addressed head-on.
perspectives_weighted.interdisciplinary: MEDIUM. The thermodynamic vocabulary provides supplementary context (ideal gas analogy, entropy production) but is not central to the contribution. The allostatic hierarchy is retained as backup. The Le Chatelier stability analysis informs the CSI metric design.
perspectives_weighted.empiricist: HIGH. The statistical protocol (paired t-test, Bonferroni, bootstrap CI, Cohen's d), the falsification criteria, the confound identification (effective WD strength, HP tuning budget, augmentation), and the phased experimental design (pilot -> CIFAR -> ImageNet) are adopted completely. This perspective ensures the paper meets NeurIPS methodological standards.
